name: sidecar
description: Claude Code plugin pack with strict concept separation. Top-level dirs: hooks/ (PreToolUseآ·SessionStart auto-behavior), commands/ (/slash-command invoked), skills/ (Skill tool invocable). 1 plugin = 1 concept; no mixing.

plugins.name: commons
plugins.description: UserPromptSubmit + SessionStart + PreCompact + PostCompact hook â€” injects a cross-project do/dont layer from commons.tape (granular `@D g0..g75` form, no tool/usage) + a live `pool` roster snapshot (~/.pool/pool.json) + a live cwd `./pods.json` per-project pod/job manifest snapshot appended per injection so the host lineup AND the /system SSOT are visible without running `pool list` / `hexa cloud pods` first; UserPromptSubmit re-injects every turn so the layer never fades; SessionStart additionally appends COMMANDS.md (the cross-project slash-command catalog) once per session. Carries LATTICE_POLICY.md (real-limits-first SSOT). 0.13.0 (2026-05-29 آ· CLOUD M10) repoints `_pods_snapshot()` to the GLOBAL M5 registry `~/.hx/cloud/active-pods.json` (cwd-independent آ· cost-bearing آ· `<id>[<provider>,<status>]` + `$<cost>`) + adds `_providers_snapshot()` (M8 catalog `~/.hx/cloud/providers.json` one-liner). Both global â†’ always visible آ· absent â†’ silent. 0.12.0 (2026-05-28) added `_pods_snapshot()` on cwd `./pods.json` (superseded by M5 global registry); pair feature with `pods-route` hook (auto `--register`). 0.11.0 (2026-05-28) drops the `enabled` per-host field + `[os,on|off]` rendering from the roster snapshot â€” roster hosts are always-on (pool 0.8.9 ships the matching always-on model on the CLI side); the SessionStart roster line now renders `name[os]` and pi5-akida exclusion is handled downstream by pool-route's name-substring akida match. 0.10.15 adds @D g75 (candidate orchestration â€” build phase vs fire phase split, domain-agnostic â€” pairs with micro-exp 0.4.0 Stage 1.5 + cycle 0.9.2 decision tree).

plugins.name: project-tape

plugins.name: git-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) + SessionStart git-staleness safety guard, in hexa-lang (`_git_guard.hexa` + `_git_guard_session.hexa`, via `hexa run`). Five layers, all no-network (FETCH_HEAD mtime is the freshness proxy â€” the hook NEVER runs `git fetch`). DOWNSTREAM: (1) DENIES force-type push â€” `git push --force` / `-f` آ· `--force-with-lease` آ· `git push <remote> +<refspec>` (refspec-level force); quotes stripped before tokenizing (0.4.2) so `'--force-with-lease=x'` / `+"refspec"` can't slip past. (2) STALE-BASE PUSH advisory (0.5.0, non-blocking): for a non-force push, emits additionalContext when the current branch trails the remote default base (origin/HEAD, else origin/main) by >= 20 commits â€” the shared-workdir branch-swap / silent-revert fingerprint; push still proceeds. (3) STALE-BASE MERGE advisory (0.6.0, anima #1105, non-blocking): a `gh pr merge` (squash) reaches main via a path the push-time check never saw â€” it revert-deleted 35190 files in #1105 â€” so when the command contains `gh pr merge` (no `git push`, not cross-repo `--repo X`), runs the same divergence probe and warns before the merge lands (real deny on mass deletion lives in pr-cycle-hook's deletion-sanity gate). UPSTREAM (0.7.0): (4) CREATION advisory â€” on `git worktree add` / `git checkout -b` / `git switch -c`, warns when the base does not resolve to `origin/<default>` (local main/HEAD/omitted) OR `.git/FETCH_HEAD` is older than 30 min; steers to `git fetch origin` then base off `origin/<default>`. (5) SessionStart advisory â€” `git rev-list --left-right --count origin/<default>...<default>` against already-fetched refs; if local default is >= 1 behind origin it nudges `git fetch origin && git pull --ff-only` (the shared-tree drift where a sibling agent merged to origin), plus a fetch nudge when FETCH_HEAD is stale; silent when up-to-date / not a repo / no origin. Hook-bypass (--no-verify) NOT blocked (left to user discipline). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: workdir-guard
plugins.description: SessionStart advisory (hexa-lang `_workdir_guard.hexa`, via `hexa run`) that fires once per session, and only when the current git working tree is shared by >= 2 concurrent `claude` agents. Detection (~0.1s): `ps` lists claude PIDs (comm == 'claude' â€” lsof's command field shows the version string, not 'claude'), then one `lsof -a -p <list> -d cwd` collects their cwds, awk prefix-matched against `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`. A shared working tree = shared `.git/index` + `HEAD`, so a sibling agent can swap HEAD out from under you, commingle your staged files, or leave your push on a stale branch. Injects a one-shot reminder to do git landings in an isolated worktree and never reset/switch/delete another agent's active branch. Grouped by working tree, not repo â€” silent in solo sessions (count 1) and self-extinguishes once you move into a worktree. Advisory-only, non-blocking. Proactive complement to git-guard's reactive stale-base push advisory. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: worktree-guard
plugins.description:
PreToolUse(Bash) advisory for `git worktree add`, in hexa-lang (`_worktree_guard.hexa`, via `hexa run`). When a Bash command creates a git worktree, emits non-blocking systemMessage with the durable-worktree drill: in a heavily-concurrent or synced repo an isolated worktree dir can be removed out from under you (a sibling agent's `git worktree prune`, a workspace sync, or the macOS /tmp reaper) and any UNCOMMITTED edits vanish with it â€” so commit + push promptly after editing, don't hold uncommitted work across turns, land via PR then `git worktree remove` (verification builds AFTER the commit, so a failed build never costs the work). Stronger note when the target path is under a volatile location (/tmp آ· /private/tmp آ· /var/folders). BRANCH-REUSE advisory (0.2.0, anima #1105): when `git worktree add -b
` reuses an existing branch â€” especially a LOCAL-only stale one absent from origin (a rejected/unpushed prior turn's leftover) â€” warns of the stale-base risk that revert-deleted 35190 files in #1105 and suggests a fresh per-PR branch (`
-$(date +%s)` from origin/main). Advisory-only (guards-narrow-scope: non-blocking systemMessage > deny for hygiene). Sister of workdir-guard (shared-tree SessionStart advisory) + worktree-gc (merged-worktree prune). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: memory-lint
plugins.description: PostToolUse(Write|Edit) advisory for the auto-memory index file (`memory/MEMORY.md`), implemented in hexa-lang (`_memory_lint.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Reads the post-write file when tool_input.file_path ends in `memory/MEMORY.md` and emits non-blocking systemMessage for: entry count > 30 (hot-tier soft cap â†’ migrate older items to sibling MEMORY.archive.md) آ· any non-comment line > 150 chars (TLDR refactor budget â€” verb + nouns, push trailing clauses into the sibling `*.md` file). Keeps the always-loaded MEMORY.md dense + greppable as memories accumulate. Advisory-only by design (per guards-narrow-scope: non-blocking systemMessage > deny for hygiene).

plugins.name: drift-guard
plugins.description: PostToolUse(Write|Edit) advisory that prevents design-drift regression â€” when a design-defining surface is edited but MEMORY.md + CLAUDE.md/project.tape are not, the next session (which loads memory at startup, not the diff) regresses to the old design. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_drift_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Fires on any of: (1) SSOT file edit â€” path ends in `.tape` or `/CLAUDE.md`; (2) a new artifact under a `decisions/` folder; (3) an explicit `@design-change` marker in the post-write content. Skips the mirror targets themselves (`/memory/`, MEMORY.md) and the guard's own install dir. Emits non-blocking systemMessage nudging the model to mirror the change into the project auto-memory (memory file + MEMORY.md pointer), or â€” for a cross-project governance rule â€” to ask the user to `sidecar sign` commons.tape/project.tape. De-dupes once per (session, file) via a CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA marker. Advisory-only. 0.1.1 â€” kills self-trigger noise: sentinel scan now limited to source-code extensions (.hexa/.py/.sh/â€¦), so prose-heavy docs (marketplace/README/CHANGELOG/SKILL) that DOCUMENT the marker no longer self-trigger on every edit; sidecar repo's own /hooks/drift-guard/ tree is now skipped (in addition to CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT cache). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: verdict-gate
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Write|Edit) hard block + Stop advisory enforcing commons @D g73 (a verdict is EARNED by independent recompute, never self-judged â€” anti-fake-closure). Strict filename gate: fires ONLY when the target basename matches `H_*.md` or equals `result.json`. Layer (A) HARD DENY â€” a Write/Edit that asserts a verdict token (ًں”µ / ًںں¢ / SUPPORTED-FORMAL / SUPPORTED-NUMERICAL) in such a file, while the backing repo-root `.verdicts/<slug>/` (slug = `H_<slug>.md` stripped of `H_`+`.md`, or the containing dir name for result.json) holds no non-empty `*.txt` (verbatim `hexa verify` stdout), is denied with a redirect to run `hexa verify` and persist the output first. Repo root = nearest ancestor with a `.git`, else cwd. Layer (B) ADVISORY (non-blocking systemMessage) â€” a `.hexa` write containing a tautological smoke (a `check`'d `fn` whose single-statement body returns the literal/param it is compared against) warns 'tautological smoke â€” not verification (g73)'. Layer (C) Stop ADVISORY â€” if recent (mtime < 6h) `H_*.md` files assert ًں”µ/ًںں¢ with 0% `.verdicts/` backing, emits a non-blocking Stop reason 'loop self-judging only آ· STOP & report (g73)'; never a hard stop-block (a hard block could trap an autonomous loop). Non-applicable tokens âڑھ SPECULATION-FENCED / ًںں، CITATION / ًںں  INSUFFICIENT NEVER fire (already honest). Plain `.md` prose mentioning ًں”µ (the RFC, commons.tape, README rubric) is NOT caught â€” the filename gate excludes it. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_verdict_gate.hexa` + `_verdict_stop.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Closes the standing commons @D s7 gap (g73 landed without its enforcement hook). NO opt-out by design (commons @D s11 â€” gated only on real preconditions: missing payload skip, filename gate, token absent skip, evidence present skip).

plugins.name: sidecar-lint
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) auto-lint that fires on `git commit` in any Claude Code marketplace plugin pack (any repo with .claude-plugin/marketplace.json), implemented in hexa-lang (`_sidecar_lint.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Non-blocking findings as additionalContext: stale-history patterns in staged diff (commons @D g15) آ· hardcoded /Users//home/ absolute paths in staged diff (commons @D g13 آ· sidecar @D s3) آ· marketplace.json plugin entry â†” each plugin.json version drift (commons @D g22) آ· hook .sh missing user-exec bit آ· missing CHANGELOG.md at repo root (commons @D g29) آ· (when .claude-plugin/profiles.json is present) any marketplace plugin missing a tier there (sidecar @D s7 â€” untagged silently defaults to 'personal', hidden from the minimal/hexa profiles) آ· any registered STDIO MCP-server plugin (project.tape @D s15 â€” `mcps/` source or command-based `mcpServers`; STDIO MCP forbidden, its stdin/stdout JSON-RPC handshake collides with hexa hook stdio + fake-stdin; an HTTP/SSE-transport `mcpServers` with a `url` is PERMITTED, not flagged). 0.9.0 â€” s15 relaxed to allow HTTP/SSE MCP (stdio still flagged). 0.8.1 â€” the top-level-dir check (project.tape @D s16) is an ALLOWLIST not a name-blocklist: any tracked top-level dir that is NOT a concept dir (`hooks`آ·`commands`آ·`skills`آ·`agents`)/`bin`/`.github`/`.claude-plugin` is flagged, so renaming a bucket (`docs`â†’`documentation`) does NOT dodge it; the advisory directs co-location under the owning domain/plugin. Tracked-only so gitignored runtime/scratch never false-fires. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: hexa-native
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Write|Edit|NotebookEdit|Bash) hard block for `.py` / `.sh` writes inside any project rooted at a directory containing a `project.tape` marker (sidecar's canonical project identity file), implemented in hexa-lang (`_hexa_native.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Covers BOTH write channels: structured file tools (Write/Edit/NotebookEdit) and the Bash write channels â€” `> X.py`, `>> X.sh`, heredoc-to-redirect (`cat > X.py << EOF`), `tee [-a] X.py`, plus non-redirect channels `dd of=X.py` and `cp`/`mv` destination (0.3.2); relative targets resolved against the payload `cwd`. Quote-aware redirects; fd-dup (`2>&1`) yields no target; non-write Bash (`python X.py`, `cat X.py`, `chmod X.sh`) passes through. Unbounded channels (`python -c open(...)`, `sed -i`, `perl -e`, `install -t`) are NOT covered â€” this narrows the common write paths, not a sandbox. Walks up parent dirs of the target; if a `project.tape` is found, the write is denied with an action-first message that leads with the fix (re-issue as `.hexa`) and demotes the refusal reason, so the reader reroutes instead of reading â€œblockedâ€‌ and punting to a doc / the user. NO opt-out: no env var, no config file, no exception list, no self-exclusion â€” hard-coded by design. Targets only `.py` / `.sh`; other extensions pass through. Projects without `project.tape` are unaffected.

plugins.name: sign-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Write|Edit|NotebookEdit|Bash) sign-gate for governance-SSOT files, implemented in hexa-lang (`_sign_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). A small registry (GATED) of high-stakes files â€” currently `commons.tape` (cross-project do/dont layer), `project.tape` (per-project identity + governance), and `.gitignore` (commit-hygiene: silently controls what enters version control, so a stealth agent edit could un-ignore a secret or hide a file from review), and `~/.sidecar/local-paths` (0.1.7 â€” the LOCAL-EXECUTION WHITELIST: a registered path-prefix silently overrides pool-route to force commands LOCAL, so a stealth add routes arbitrary commands off the pool) â€” may be edited by the agent ONLY while a fresh user sign-off token exists. The user mints one with `sidecar sign <key>` (`commons` آ· `project` آ· `gitignore` آ· `paths`), writing the current epoch to `~/.sidecar/signs/<key>.sign`; a token is valid for 300s (5 min). Covers both write channels (mirrors hexa-native): structured file tools and the Bash write channels (`> X`, `>> X`, heredoc, `tee X`, `dd of=X`, `cp`/`mv` dest; relative targets resolved against `cwd`). Writes buried inside an invoked CLI (e.g. `sidecar init`) are not visible at the top-level command string, so the official scaffolder is unaffected â€” only the agent's own direct writes gate. The hook CODE-ENFORCES the no-self-mint rule: it hard-denies the agent's mint command `sidecar sign <key>` (list / clear stay allowed) AND any write forging a token under `~/.sidecar/signs/`. Because the agent's tool calls (Bash آ· Write/Edit) route through this hook, the agent CANNOT self-mint. Mint detection (0.1.5) is narrowed to a real command invocation: the Bash channel is scanned on a quote-masked copy and sidecar must sit at a command position (first token or right after a shell separator), so a sidecar sign <key> MENTION inside a quoted commit message or PR body is no longer mis-flagged - pre-0.1.5 it wrongly blocked legit commits that referenced the command. (The interactive `!` bang a USER types in the TUI prompt does NOT route through the hook and mints freely â€” the intended user path, safe because the agent cannot emit a `!` bang.) `bin/sidecar` carries an OPT-IN /dev/tty second layer (`SIDECAR_SIGN_REQUIRE_TTY=1`) defeating hook-parser-evading obfuscation. The fresh token is the sole precondition (project.tape @D s11: gate on a real precondition, not an opt-out switch); enforcement is the hard deny (project.tape @D s13). To gate another file: add one row to GATED and reship. NO env-var / flag override by design.

plugins.name: plist-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Write|Edit|NotebookEdit) hard block for `.plist` writes, implemented in hexa-lang (`_plist_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Any target file ending in `.plist` (LaunchAgents آ· LaunchDaemons آ· Info.plist آ· UserDefaults) is denied with a redirecting message â€” the agent cannot distinguish user-explicit from model-autonomous intent, so all .plist writes are refused globally; if the user truly wants one, they run the command themselves outside the agent path. LaunchAgents / LaunchDaemons are persistence surfaces (auto-launch on login / boot), so silent writes risk installing daemons the user did not ask for. Enforces commons @D g37 (plist user-request only). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: cloud-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) hard block for raw rented-GPU pod dispatch (commons @D g8). Four orthogonal detectors run on every Bash command: (a) pod-CLI + remote-verb pair scan anywhere in the command â€” runpodctl {exec|ssh|send|receive|send-file|receive-file|send-file-to-pod|receive-file-from-pod|port} آ· vast(ai) {exec|ssh|scp|copy|attach-ssh|execute}; (b) ssh/scp/rsync/sftp against `*.runpod.io` / `proxy.runpod.net` / `*.vast.ai` hosts; (c) curl/wget/http(ie)/xh against `api.runpod.io` / `api.runpod.ai` / `rest.runpod.io` / `runpod.io/graphql` / `console.vast.ai` / `vast.ai/api` URLs; (d) dispatcher-script content scan â€” `bash/sh/zsh/dash <path>` reads the script and re-runs detectors (a)/(b)/(c) on its body, closing the workaround of pre-writing a dispatch.sh with raw `runpodctl ssh` and invoking it. Shell separators normalized to whitespace before tokenizing â€” `cd /x && runpodctl ssh â€¦`, `bash -c 'â€¦ vast exec â€¦'`, env-prefixes all caught. Pod-lifecycle verbs (create / get / start / stop / remove / show / search / launch / destroy / config / project / version) pass through. Redirects to `hexa cloud {run|nohup|poll|copy-to|copy-from|copy-dir-*|preflight}`. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_cloud_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: pod-monitor
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) advisory hook for GPU pod fires (`hexa cloud nohup` / `hexa cloud fire` / `hexa cloud run`). Emits non-blocking systemMessage reminding the agent of commons @D g57 â€” four invariants: set `SAVE_POD=1` envar (pod retain when SSH drops), attach the ًں›°ï¸ڈ Monitor tool to the streaming log (no `tail -f` / sleep-poll), âڈ± estimate walltime up front with pod-hours headroom (undersize â†’ pod auto-killed mid-run â†’ result LOST), AND ًں“‹ register the fired pod in cwd's `./pods.json` via `hexa cloud dispatch add <jid> <pid> <dir>` (cloud@0.4.0 + hexa-lang PR #1699 آ· ghost-pod class â€” caller fires but never registers â†’ per-project ledger / billing / verdict lost). Closes the R8a-LOST class + the ghost-pod class. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_pod_monitor.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). 0.1.4 â€” pods.json ledger hint added (caller-side responsibility; hexa cloud fire/nohup does NOT auto-attach upstream-side). 0.1.3 â€” `_has_pod_fire` uses token-position match past env-var prefix (the cloud verb must SIT at command position, not be a substring of the cmd string) so commit messages / grep patterns / docs that merely mention the phrase no longer trigger noise. `fire` verb (hexa-lang PR #1309) added. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: monitor-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) advisory hook for background / long-running shell launches (trailing `&` آ· `nohup`/`setsid`/`disown` آ· the Bash tool's `run_in_background` flag). When the rate-limit-survivable pattern is incomplete, emits non-blocking systemMessage enforcing commons @D g10 â€” the while-monitor pattern: (1) detach the job (`nohup`/`setsid`) so a session rate-limit death does NOT SIGHUP it, (2) write a progress log (`> /tmp/<job>.log 2>&1`) as a durable record + Monitor re-attach point, (3) attach the ًں›°ï¸ڈ Monitor tool to the LOG (not the live pipe â€” token-light, re-attachable after a rate-limit), with an OS-side `while` heartbeat for liveness (no model-side sleep-poll). Stays silent when the job is already detached AND logged. Closes the rate-limit-force-kill class for general local background work; general-local sister of pod-monitor (GPU pods) and proactive sister of limit-guard (post-hoc checkpoint). Implemented in hexa-lang (`_monitor_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). 0.1.1 â€” `hexa cloud nohup`/`hexa cloud fire` patterns ALWAYS fire a cloud-bridge hint (basic detach+log is inherent to the verb, but the cloud workflow has a separate requirement: thread the logfile path from nohup into `hexa cloud tail` so Monitor can attach). 0.1.2 â€” the `hexa cloud fire` verb + `__MONITOR_HANDLE__={â€¦}` JSON-line contract are now SHIPPED upstream (hexa-lang PR #1306 + #1309); cloud-bridge hint points to the live atomic workflow (`hexa cloud fire <host> -- <argv>` â†’ grep handle line â†’ attach Monitor to `tail_cmd`) instead of the pre-ship 3-step manual handoff. 0.1.3 â€” cloud-bridge trigger now uses token-position match past env-var prefix (`_is_cloud_pod`) instead of `cmd.contains(...)` substring â€” kills the noise where commit messages / grep patterns merely mentioning the phrase fired the advisory. Also fires the hint even when `is_bg` would short-circuit (cloud fire/nohup are inherently dispatch verbs). 0.1.4 â€” `has_detach` follows the same token-position pattern via `_has_detach_pos`. 0.1.5 â€” `has_log` also via `_has_log_pos` + `_redirect_targets` (quote-aware extraction of `>`/`>>` redirect targets, `tee` invocations with file args, `<<` heredocs) â€” commands that merely MENTION `.log` in an unrelated arg (e.g. `grep nohup /var/log/foo.log`) no longer satisfy the log-sink check incorrectly. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: throttle-guard
plugins.description: PostToolUse(Task|Agent) cross-session coordinator for the TRANSIENT server throttle â€” 'Server is temporarily limiting requests (not your usage limit) آ· Rate limited' â€” that fires when too many concurrent requests hit at once (several sessions, or a wide fan-out). Distinct from limit-guard (the 5h/7d USAGE/session cap â†’ checkpoint + RESUME). On detect: (1) writes/extends a SHARED cooldown marker ~/.sidecar/throttle.json with an exponential backoff (15آ·30آ·60آ·120آ·240آ·300s, capped, 120s storm window) so every session/round reads ONE backoff window â€” killing the thundering-herd re-fire where all sessions retry in lockstep and re-trip the throttle; (2) injects a non-blocking directive â€” back off with JITTER (never retry in lockstep), cut fan-out WIDTH to â‰¤1 until the window passes, resume by re-reading not re-running. Concurrency control that RESPECTS the throttle (clears once in-flight load drops), not evasion. Gated to NOT double-fire with limit-guard (skips when the result names a usage/session limit). Implemented in hexa-lang (`_throttle_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: ai-api-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) hard block for raw AI-API calls when a sidecar CLI wraps the same operation, implemented in hexa-lang (`_ai_api_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Two deny patterns: (1) `curl`/`wget`/`http`/`httpie` to an AI hostname (`api.openai.com` آ· `api.anthropic.com` آ· `fal.run` آ· `api.replicate.com` آ· `generativelanguage.googleapis.com`); (2) inline `python(3)?` / `node` / `deno` / `bun` with `-c`/`-e` AND `import` of `openai` / `anthropic` / `fal_client` / `replicate` / `google.generativeai`. Redirects to `/imagine` (image) / `/research` (text) / etc. â€” commons @D g50 enforcement. Whitespace/line-continuation is normalized before matching (0.1.3) so multi-space / split forms still match; dynamic imports (`exec(...)`, base64) remain out of scope (static scan limit). General SDK usage in committed code files (no inline `-c`/`-e`) passes through. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: verify-guard
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) hard block for raw verification-tool usage cited as primary evidence, implemented in hexa-lang (`_verify_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Denies first-token âˆˆ {wolframscript, mathematica, WolframKernel}, and inline-Python verification via `python(3)? -c '...'` containing `import sympy` / `from sympy` / `import pyphi` / `import wolframclient` with a redirecting message pointing to `hexa verify` (commons @D g5). General sympy / wolframclient usage in committed code files passes through â€” only inline `-c` verification probes (and the standalone Wolfram CLIs) are refused. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: pr-cycle
plugins.description: /pr-cycle â€” one-shot PR cycle command. Pushes the current branch (`git push -u origin HEAD`) then `gh pr create --fill`; the `pr-cycle-hook` plugin's PreToolUse hook then auto-appends ` && gh pr merge --squash --admin --delete-branch` (+ worktree/branch cleanup when run from a linked worktree) â€” one command does create â†’ merge â†’ clean. Refuses on main/master. Pass extra gh flags as args. Command half of the pr-cycle split (routing hook = `pr-cycle-hook`).

plugins.name: pr-cycle-hook
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Bash) PR full-cycle router for `gh pr create`, in hexa-lang (`_pr_cycle.hexa`, via `hexa run`). Quote-aware tokenizer scans for the `gh pr create` 3-gram anywhere â€” catches bare and chained (`git push && gh pr create â€¦`) forms. When matched (not draft / not already merging), rewrites via the `updatedInput` field (emitted with `permissionDecision: allow` in the same hookSpecificOutput â€” REQUIRED or the rewrite is ignored) to append the merge tail and, IF cwd is a linked worktree, ` && (cd <main-wt> && git worktree remove <this-wt> --force; git branch -D <branch>)` â€” atomic create â†’ merge â†’ worktree+branch cleanup. Cross-repo `--repo X` routes the merge to X, skips local cleanup. 0.3.0 â€” explicit PR# capture via `gh pr view --json number -q .number` (cross-repo via `gh pr view --repo X`); robust when branch-ref is detached / worktree mid-teardown / CI strips head-ref; cross-repo pins both view+merge to the target repo. DELETION-SANITY GATE (0.2.0, anima #1105): before appending the auto-merge, probes the squash payload (`git diff --name-status <merge-base(origin/main,HEAD)>...HEAD`, or `gh pr diff` cross-repo) for a mass-deletion outlier â€” D-count > 50, OR deletions >= 10x additions and > 20 abs â€” and DENIES the merge (PR stays created, the dangerous squash-merge is withheld) to block stale-base regressions like the 35190-file revert-delete. Fail-open on probe failure. Enforces commons @D g47. Skip: `--draft` آ· already contains `gh pr merge`. Hook half of the pr-cycle split. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: tape-lint
plugins.description: PreToolUse(Edit|Write) deny for `.tape` edits, implemented in hexa-lang (`_tape_lint.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run` â€” no Python, no shell shim). Four checks, all diff-aware (pre-existing violations grandfathered, only newly-introduced or worsened items block). (1) fields rule â€” `@D` blocks accept only `do` / `dont`; new `why` آ· `tool` آ· `note` آ· `ref` آ· `ex` آ· ... refused. Fires on any `*.tape` path. (2) length cap â€” `do` / `dont` value > 100 chars refused (split overflow). commons.tape + project.tape only. (3) authoring-language â€” when `sidecar prefs` `code` axis is `english`, lines newly introducing non-Latin scripts (Hangul آ· CJK آ· Hiragana آ· Katakana) are refused. commons.tape + project.tape only. (4) @I siblings â€” project.tape's @I block must carry a `siblings` field; dropping it or adding a fresh @I without it is refused (cross-repo network SSOT). project.tape only. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: pool-route
plugins.description: 0.14.0 SIDECAR CLI LOCAL-BOUND (routing bug fix): the bare `sidecar <verb>` CLI joins the local-bound early-exit whitelist alongside `git`/`gh`/`pool` (`_has_word(cmd, "sidecar")`). The `sidecar` marketplace CLI is host-LOCAL â€” it manages `~/.sidecar/` (handoff registry آ· signs آ· local-paths whitelist آ· profiles) + `~/.claude/` (installed_plugins.json آ· enabledPlugins) â€” so `sidecar handoff add â€¦` / `sidecar sync` mis-routed to a pool host (ubu-2) wrote the WRONG machine's state (or 127'd where no `sidecar` binary exists). The bare CLI was NOT covered pre-fix: the $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA literal pin (0.6.8) + the `~/.sidecar` dotstate pin (0.6.9) only catch commands literally carrying those tokens, and `sidecar handoff add <repo> <text>` carries neither. Mirrors the `pool` pin exactly (sidecar-family local CLI آ· @D s10 local-bound cmds stay local). 0.13.0 MINI-EQUAL TOGGLE DROPPED (user feature 2026-05-28): the `~/.pool/mini-equal` marker is no longer read â€” general-heavy round-robin is permanently Linux-only (Mac stays the workstation per @D s12). macOS pool hosts (mini) still receive macOS-only auto-dispatches (swift / xcodebuild / codesign / â€¦); explicit `pool on mini <cmd>` is the manual escape for ad-hoc macOS work. Companion ship: pool 0.8.10 (drops `pool mini-equal` + `pool clean on|off` verbs + the `clean` per-host field). Together with 0.12.0 this retires the last on/off-style toggles in the pool stack. 0.12.0 ALWAYS-ON ROSTER (user feature 2026-05-28): the per-host `enabled` field is no longer read â€” every roster host listed in `~/.pool/pool.json` is treated as on for routing-candidate purposes. Drops the residual `enabled=true` filter at the head of the eligibility scan; the existing pi5-akida name-substring exclusion (anima-only آ· @D s8) is the ONLY remaining gate, so behavior is identical for the current roster (mini آ· ubu-1 آ· ubu-2 routing-eligible, pi5-akida excluded) but the model and `pool list` are no longer cluttered with an on/off toggle that never actually toggled. Companion ships: pool 0.8.9 (drops `shared:false` field + `[ded]` marker آ· akida exclusion via the same name-substring helper) + sidecar commons 0.11.0 (SessionStart roster snapshot renders `name[os]` instead of `name[os,on|off]`). 0.11.0 FORK-STORM CAP (user feature 2026-05-28): a whitelist-forced-LOCAL hexa run escapes the default hexaâ†’pool offload and runs on the Mac; when FORK_STORM_CAP (default 8) or more USER hexa interpreters are already live locally, a NEW whitelist-local hexa is DENIED with a runbook â€” locality preserved (imports still resolve), storm capped. Counts via `ps -Ao args` (first-token basename `hexa`, one line per run), excludes sidecar's own hooks (`cache/sidecar`) + `claude` (@D s9), fails OPEN on a count error so it never blocks (@D s11). 0.10.0 SIDECAR PATH-PREFIX WHITELIST (user feature â€” chat-form sbs FULL agreement 2026-05-28): a new permanent LOCAL-EXECUTION exemption layer reads `~/.sidecar/local-paths` (line-based plain text, USER-managed via the new `sidecar paths {bare|add|rm}` CLI verb) â€” any Bash cmd whose argv contains a `/`-prefixed absolute-path token that starts with a registered prefix is FORCED LOCAL (skip pool routing). PERMANENT counterpart to the runtime `sign-local` token (which is one-shot 30-min); no TTL. The hook scans ALL argv tokens, tolerates a missing file (empty whitelist آ· no fail-closed آ· no caching since the file is typically 2-10 lines), and skips blank + `#` comment lines. Seeded on first use by `sidecar paths` with sidecar's own cache + repo paths (`~/.claude/plugins/cache/sidecar/` + `~/core/sidecar/`) so sidecar's own hexa hooks/commands stop being routed to the pool â€” closes the recurring fail mode where the cache (workstation-only) was sent to a pool peer. Sits between host-introspection and the heavy classifier in the routing decision; emits a one-line `pool-route: local (sidecar path whitelist آ· prefix=<matched>)` on hit. The whitelist itself IS the opt-in â€” no env opt-out (project.tape @D s11). Companion CLI: `sidecar paths` lists, `sidecar paths add` registers cwd, `sidecar paths add <dir>` registers an abs-resolved dir (refuses non-existent / already-present), `sidecar paths rm <prefix>` removes a line (refuses if not found). 0.9.3 DATA-LOCALITY PIN + SIGN-LOCAL TTL 30min (anima PURE F-CURRICULA-1): a local-data-dependent `hexa run` (Mac-local corpus input) was blocked on BOTH routing paths. (a) data-locality pin (5th structural LOCAL-EXECUTION exemption): a hexa exec whose data-INPUT flag (--corpus-path/--corpus/--data/--data-path/--dataset/--input/--train-data/--eval-data/--weights/--ckpt/--checkpoint) points at a LOCAL-ONLY path (anything NOT under a synced ~/core/ workdir) now PINS LOCAL â€” the pool peer has no copy of that data, so routing reproduced the observed failure (`hexa run ... --corpus-path /Users/.../c.jsonl` to ubu-2, compile-stage `source not found` = a data-absence error misread as a code error). Synced ~/core data still routes (peer mirrors it). Same data-locality necessity as the host-introspection whitelist; quote-stripped tokens; unconditional, no opt-out env (s11). (b) sign-local TTL raised 5min to 30min (LOCAL_SIGN_TTL=1800 in _local_signed()): a single legitimate local build (corpus regen + compile) runs 10-20min, exceeding the old 5min `! sidecar sign local` window, so a mid-build `hexa run` re-tripped the fork-storm sign gate on token expiry (re-sign every 5min was unworkable; F-CURRICULA-1 fire BLOCKED twice). TRADE-OFF: the fork-storm guard window is now 6x longer, accepted (the local token is explicit, user-minted with no agent self-mint, single-key, clearable early via `sidecar sign clear local`); a build-covering window is the Occam fix (g0) vs a sliding-window/session-marker mechanism. The local token TTL is INDEPENDENT of the governance sign TTL (SIGN_TTL=300 in bin/sidecar + hooks/sign-guard, gating agent EDITS to commons/project/gitignore, stays 5min); bin/sidecar mint message + --list are now key-aware (sign_ttl_for: local=1800, governance=300). 0.9.1 ROLLBACK of 0.8.3 canon: each pool host's hexa binary has host-specific path-resolution (ubu-1 = bash wrapper exec'ing hexa_real; ubu-2 = ELF binary with argv[0]-relative `self/native/hexat` search آ· symlinks were Mac-paths broken on Linux). HEXA_REAL_BIN/HEXA_LANG=canon injection works on some hosts, silently breaks on others (qwen_bpe.hexa `run` needs `self/native/hexat` at a host-specific path canon doesn't supply consistently). 0.9.1 reverts to host-owns-its-hexa: pool-route uses host's `~/core/hexa-lang` for `HEXA_LANG` and host's PATH-resolved `hexa` binary. Stale-stdlib is the host owner's responsibility (anima for ~/core/hexa-lang drift on ubu-2). `~/.hx/canon` + canon-update.sh remain as scaffolding on hosts where they were bootstrapped; pool-route no longer routes through canon. POLICY INVERSION (0.9.0 â€” ىœ ى € directive "ë¬´ى،°ê±´ pool آ· ي™”ى‌´يٹ¸ë¦¬ىٹ¤يٹ¸ë§Œ local ê°€ëٹ¥"): default = POOL. Only LOCAL-EXECUTION WHITELIST hits stay local â€” heavy-vs-light gating + default-local fallthrough REMOVED. Whitelist (closed list, sign-gated additions): sidecar self-hooks ($CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/DATA) آ· hexa cloud آ· hexa atlas register آ· git/gh آ· /Users//home abs-path heavy-interp (sign-gated) آ· ~/. dotstate آ· npm/pnpm/yarn آ· pool CLI آ· HOST-INTROSPECTION (0.9.0 new â€” ps آ· top آ· uptime آ· w آ· who آ· df آ· du آ· free آ· vmstat آ· iostat آ· uname آ· sysctl آ· launchctl آ· systemctl آ· pgrep آ· pkill آ· kill آ· killall آ· stat آ· lsof آ· netstat آ· ifconfig آ· ip آ· arp آ· route آ· dscl آ· scutil آ· sw_vers آ· first-token basename match آ· semantic-necessity not convenience) آ· `! sidecar sign local` 5min token. Pre-0.9.0 heavy classifier retained for OS-capability hints only (is_macos/is_linux â†’ which pool host). The `find /` root-tree case (anima 100%+ CPU 2:44+) now routes by default. SSH overhead accepted. TERMINOLOGY (0.8.4): 'WHITELIST' = LOCAL-EXECUTION allowlist (things that escape pool dispatch آ· stay local) â€” the 0.8.0 'HEXA WHITELIST' phrasing was misleading and the rule was renamed to HEXA POOL-DISPATCH. Real whitelist members: $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/DATA آ· hexa cloud آ· hexa atlas register آ· /Users//home abs-path heavy interp (sign-gated) آ· ~/. dotstate آ· git/gh آ· npm/pnpm/yarn آ· 5min `! sidecar sign local` runtime token. Registration onto the whitelist is sign-gated by design (commons @D s13 for hardcoded structural exemptions via project.tape آ· TUI `! sidecar sign local` for runtime force-local آ· no agent self-mint path). No env-var opt-out (commons @D s11). Pool-canonical hexa routing (0.8.3): pool dispatches pin hexa to `~/.hx/canon` (pool-owned origin/main checkout, independent of each host's `~/core/hexa-lang` which may carry feature branches / behind state / anima WIP). Wrapper-injection via `HEXA_REAL_BIN=$HOME/.hx/canon/build/hexa_linux` + `HEXA_LANG=$HOME/.hx/canon` + `HEXA_DAEMON=0`. Bootstrap / refresh via `~/.hx/canon-update.sh` (idempotent: git fetch origin main + reset --hard + copy bootstrap C from ~/core/hexa-lang + hexa cc --regen). Solves the stale-compiler / qwen_bpe miscompile class (ubu-2 was 614 commits behind on stale main + DNS-broken â†’ fetch impossible; canon is independent of that drift). Probe: canon binary exists + wrapper executes `hexa --help` through canon. Hosts missing canon fail preflight with a runbook deny-trailer. HEXA WHITELIST (0.8.0 â€” POOL-OFFLOAD m8): ANY hexa execution (hexa/hexa.real/hexac/hexadrv/hxv2 as the real first-token after env-prefix+wrapper+backslash unwrap) routes to the pool â€” full stop, replacing the heavy-VERB blacklist that leaked verify/parse/atlas/honesty â†’ multi-hundred-process Mac fork-storm. Only 4 STRUCTURAL exemptions stay local (handled earlier, never reach the whitelist): $CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA hexa (sidecar's own hooks â€” routing the router is incoherent), `hexa cloud` (itself a dispatcher), /Users//home/~/. abs-path hexa (sign-gated, path may be absent on peer), and `hexa atlas register` (0.8.1 â€” mutates local SSOT embedded.gen.hexa + commits locally; routing breaks the share workflow â€” only the register WRITE subverb, read forms still route). The legacy heavy_words/heavy_pairs classifier remains for NON-hexa heavy compute (make/cargo/gcc/node/java/ffmpeg/â€¦). When the `pool` CLI's roster (`~/.pool/pool.json`) has â‰¥1 enabled host and a Bash command matches the hexa whitelist or the non-hexa heavy classifier (make آ· cargo آ· go build آ· swift build آ· docker build آ· pytest آ· nvidia-smi آ· train آ· hexa kick/drill/loop/cc/run/build آ· aprime_cc آ· `find $HOME/core/anima` big-tree scan آ· â€¦) or a root-needing command, it is rewritten via the hook `updatedInput` field (emitted with `permissionDecision: allow` in the same hookSpecificOutput) to run on a pool host over ssh â€” transparent dispatch, not a suggestion. Routing is EXPLICIT-classifier only: the load-escalation gate (promote any non-trivial cmd to heavy on high summed non-claude CPU) is absent because on a multi-core Mac that threshold trips constantly and ships local-bound git/hexa-run/smoke commands to Linux hosts where their paths don't exist. Local-bound guard (extended 0.6.9): `git`/`gh` commands, any `/Users/`آ·`/home/` absolute-path literal, and any `~/.`آ·`$HOME/.` home-dotstate reference always run local â€” dotstate forms (`~/.zsh_history`آ·`~/.hexa-cache`آ·`~/.pool`آ·`~/.sidecar`آ·â€¦) are per-host hidden state, not user-synced, so routing them reads the WRONG host's history/cache; `~/core/...` synced workdirs still route. Sidecar plugin pin (0.6.8): cmds carrying `$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` or `$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA` literals always run local â€” they resolve via the dispatching bash to the workstation's plugin cache, which only exists on the local Mac; routing them fails (cache absent on the pool host). Trusted surface (every sidecar slash command writes its dispatch via `$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` per @D s3), so no sign gate. Local-bound sign gate: within the `/Users/`آ·`/home/` exemption, HEAVY invocations (hexa CLI any verb آ· direct `hexa.real` آ· python/python3/py آ· gcc/g++/clang/clang++/cc/c++ آ· `bash`/`sh <script>`) require a fresh `local` sign-off token â€” the canonical mac fork-storm trigger; USER mints with `! sidecar sign local` in the TUI prompt. Bare absolute-path reads (ls/cat/grep/find/...) stay free â€” the gate narrows to interpreters/compilers only. macOS pool-host enable (0.6.6 â€” supersedes 0.6.1 Zero-macOS-offload): macOS pool hosts (e.g. `mini` with `os: macos`) are now auto-dispatch candidates for macOS-only commands (swift build آ· xcodebuild آ· codesign آ· â€¦); general-heavy round-robin still excludes macOS hosts so generic build/test fan-out stays off the workstation. Heavy-classified commands MUST use pool resources: the hook iterates every eligible host through a preflight (workdir present + the command's toolchain actually runnable - `command -v hexa` + `test -x build/hexa_interp` + `hexa <verb> --help` for hexa, `command -v <compiler>` for C/C++, 0.6.13), routes to the first that passes, and if ALL fail emits a `permissionDecision: deny` with a per-host failure breakdown â€” never silently falls back to local. `hexa kick`/`drill`/`loop`/`cc` get `HEXA_LANG=$HOME/core/hexa-lang`; other hexa commands get `HEXA_LANG=$PWD`. PATH + `HEXA_MODULE_LOADER` env passthrough for bare ssh shells. Each routing decision appends one JSON line to `~/.pool/route-log.jsonl` (capped at 100); SessionStart reads the last 5 back as additionalContext. Cloud pin (0.6.10): `hexa cloud *` always runs local â€” it is ITSELF a Mac-local-only remote-dispatch tool, and its post-`--` remote argv (nvidia-smi آ· train.log آ· make) tripped the classifier, non-deterministically shipping `hexa cloud exec <pod> -- ...` to a Linux host with no `cloud` subcommand; double-routing a dispatcher is incoherent. Worktreeâ†’canonical-root fallback (0.6.10): a heavy command in a git LINKED worktree outside $HOME (e.g. /tmp/wt-x) previously DENIED; it now resolves the MAIN checkout via `git worktree list --porcelain` and mirrors that root when it is under $HOME (a synced ~/core workdir). Runs ONLY in the previously-denying branch, so it cannot regress a routing command. C/C++ compiler routing (0.6.11): the C/C++ compiler drivers (gcc / g++ / clang / clang++ / cc / c++) join the heavy classifier - a C/C++ compile is heavy local compute, same workstation-protection rationale as make/cmake/cargo; they carry no macOS-capability marker, so they round-robin across Linux pool hosts only (the Mac stays the workstation, per @D s12). Absolute-path compiler sign gate (0.6.12): the C/C++ compiler drivers also join the local-bound sign gate within the absolute-path exemption - an absolute-path compile (e.g. /usr/bin/gcc on a /Users path) is the same mac fork-storm trigger as a heavy interpreter, so it now needs a fresh local sign-off token (! sidecar sign local) like hexa/python/sh; relative-path compiles in synced workdirs still route to the pool. First-token basename match keeps it precise. Toolchain preflight + akida exclusion (0.6.13): (a) pi5-akida is excluded from the auto-dispatch candidate set entirely (@D s8 - anima-only; reached only via explicit `pool on pi5-akida`). (b) the host preflight now verifies the command's actual toolchain, not just the workdir - every hexa verb probes command -v hexa + test -x ~/core/hexa-lang/build/hexa_interp + hexa <verb> --help, and a C/C++ compile probes command -v <compiler>; a host that fails is SKIPPED so round-robin moves on instead of routing-then-failing (the ubu-1 broken-hexa trap). Known limit (@D d6): a binary that passes --help but segfaults at runtime is not caught. mini-equal toggle (0.7.0): `pool mini-equal on` (writes ~/.pool/mini-equal = "on") opts macOS pool hosts (mini) INTO the OS-agnostic general-heavy round-robin as equal workers; is_linux commands still route Linux-only (the toggle only redistributes portable work). Default OFF preserves zero-macOS-offload (@D s12). Not an in-guard opt-out (@D s11) â€” it opens routing to an idle host, does not disable a guard. Single local-execution gate (0.7.2): a fresh `local` sign token (`! sidecar sign local`, 5min) forces EVERY command local â€” the one deliberate, user-minted 'run here, do NOT route' path, checked at the top before classification. When the router mis-classifies a local-bound command (diagnostic `ps`/`awk`, a local-fs fix) and ships it to a peer host, the user signs ONCE and routing is suppressed for the window. Subsumes the abs-path-heavy sign gate (signed â†’ top-level returns first; unsigned â†’ that gate still bites). A sanctioned time-boxed user-minted token, not an env-var opt-out (@D s11). Env-prefix + wrapper hardening (0.7.3): `_local_heavy_interp` now strips POSIX env-var prefix (`POOL_DISABLE=1`/`SIDECAR_POOL_DISABLE=1`/`FOO=bar`/â€¦, any IDENT=val â€” parser correctness, not specific-var recognition) AND known leading-position wrappers (`env` آ· `exec` آ· `nice` آ· `timeout` آ· `command` آ· `sudo` آ· `nohup` آ· `stdbuf` آ· `ionice`, with per-wrapper option-arg awareness) AND a leading backslash escape (`\hexa`) before reading the real verb â€” pre-0.7.3 any env-var prefix OR any of those wrappers silently ALLOW'd abs-path heavy invocations, breaking sign-local's single-gate integrity. `eval` quoted-string args remain out of scope (require a real shell parser). Node-package installer pin (0.7.4): npm/pnpm/yarn run LOCAL (cwd-dependent, removed from heavy_words + added to local-bound). Routing-rate baseline counters (0.7.5 â€” POOL-OFFLOAD m1): every meaningful classifier verdict increments one of 4 counters in ~/.pool/route-counters.tally (routed آ· local_bound آ· light_allowed آ· heavy_failed) so /check reports the % offloaded vs local. Non-classifier exits NOT counted. Race-tolerant. Useless env-prefix advisory (0.7.11 â€” agent education): SIDECAR_NO_POOL/POOL_DISABLE env-prefix detection at the head of a Bash cmd emits inline additionalContext advisory explaining no-op + pointing to `! sidecar sign local`. Routing unchanged; purely educational so agent stops re-trying silently. big-tree find generalization (0.7.10 â€” POOL-OFFLOAD m5): heavy_find_subs generalized from single `find $HOME/core/anima` to BOTH `find <HOME>/core/` AND `find ~/core/` (tilde form); routes ANY ~/core/* sibling repo find. Preflight test -d still gates each host. Media tools (0.7.9 â€” POOL-OFFLOAD m4): heavy_words +2 (ffmpeg/magick) + abs-path first-token sign-gate +4 (ffmpeg/magick/convert/sox آ· convert/sox English-word substring-collide â†’ first-token-only). JVM compilers + sbt (0.7.8 â€” POOL-OFFLOAD m3): heavy_words +3 (javac/kotlinc/scalac) + heavy_pairs +4 (java -jar/sbt compile/sbt test/sbt run آ· pair-only for short names) + abs-path first-token sign-gate +5 (java/javac/kotlinc/scalac/sbt). Node ecosystem (0.7.7 â€” POOL-OFFLOAD m2): heavy_words +4 (node/deno/bun/ts-node) + abs-path first-token sign-gate adds same +tsx (tsx excluded from heavy_words due to .tsx file-ext collision); mirrors python/hexa fork-storm protection. Heavy hexa verb sweep (0.7.6 â€” POOL-OFFLOAD m6): heavy_pairs +19 verbs â€” toolchain (hexa test آ· hexa bench) آ· math verifiers (hexa absolute آ· hexa meta-closure) آ· math discovery (hexa smash آ· hexa free آ· hexa hyperarithmetic) آ· discovery extras (hexa chain) آ· 11 drill variants (omega آ· surge آ· dream آ· swarm آ· reign آ· molt آ· wake آ· forge آ· canon آ· debate آ· revive) â€” all fork-storm class per the documented hexa 80+ verb catalog. Light verbs (atlas read/lookup آ· 29 annotation analyzers آ· 16 HTTP data bridges آ· lattice verify آ· gpu lint/disasm) excluded; 3-token verbs (hexa gpu fire آ· sim-universe آ· qmirror آ· atlas register --from-drill) deferred until richer classifier â€” NOTE: superseded by the 0.8.0 hexa whitelist, which routes ALL of these (except `atlas register`, pinned local in 0.8.1). plugins.name: cycle
plugins.description: Autonomous work-loop driver scoped to the session's ACTIVE DOMAIN (commons @D g58). The WHOLE family now auto-continues to DEPLETION: each round self-feeds from open milestones (else auto-seeds from `## deferred`), self-continues via ScheduleWakeup, and terminates only when open milestones = 0 AND deferred empty AND no other signal â€” so every command digs the declared backlog to the bottom on its own (per-round cap throttles WIDTH, auto-continue provides DEPTH; user can interrupt any round). The commands: /cycle â€” read the active <NAME>.md snapshot, enumerate its open `- [ ]` milestones (off-domain work is forbidden; if no active domain, prompt for `/domain set <NAME>` first); if the next-list is empty, auto-seed â‰¤N (default 3) candidate milestones â€” PRIMARY signal = the domain's OWN `## deferred` section (promote the next batch into the milestone board + drain it from deferred so the domain self-feeds its declared backlog), then user hint آ· prior-turn /gap shortlist آ· log-tail open thread â€” via `/domain milestone <text>` per item, then re-enumerate (no-signal case = zero milestones + empty deferred + no signal stops with steer-options rather than fabricating); â†’ dup-race precheck (3-signal grep: `sidecar handoff` registry entry status + merged PR + git log) â†’ parallel-plan table â†’ fan-out (one background Agent per item, same message) â†’ auto-continue to depletion (depletion-driving by default, not single-round-then-stop). /cycle-full â€” one-time phase-0 depletion brainstorm (rounds until no novel ideas, cap 8) on $ARGUMENTS within the active domain, then the same precheck â†’ plan-table â†’ fan-out â†’ auto-continue tail (phase 0 is one-time per goal; subsequent rounds use plain /cycle semantics, self-feeding from deferred, draining to depletion). /cycle-fg-loop â€” /cycle-fg handed to the `loop` skill for explicit continuous FOREGROUND-SEQUENTIAL intent + dynamic ScheduleWakeup pacing; the continuous-loop version of single-round /cycle-fg (each round runs inline one-at-a-time, â–¶ i/N â†’ âœ…/âڑ /â‌Œ, no Agent fan-out, halt on failure); same depletion termination. /cycle-bg-loop â€” /cycle-bg handed to the `loop` skill for explicit continuous BACKGROUND-PARALLEL intent + dynamic ScheduleWakeup pacing; the continuous-loop version of single-round /cycle-bg (each round fans out one background Agent per item, resource-serialized); same depletion termination. (Bare /cycle reaches the same end-state inline, dispatching fg/bg per the sticky ~/.sidecar/cycle-mode marker â€” the -loop commands are the explicit-pacing surface that names the execution mode.) /cycle-full-loop â€” /cycle-full once (one-time brainstorm + first fan-out) then handed to /loop /cycle, draining to the same depletion condition. /cycle-all (0.8.2) â€” like /cycle but with TWO overrides: NO per-round cap (enumerate the FULL open-milestone set + promote the ENTIRE `## deferred` backlog in one round, the `cap>8â†’confirm` guardrail waived) and NO recommend/select gate (do NOT print a 'recommended priority' shortlist or a 'which to dispatch?' hand-back â€” fan out EVERY PROCEED row immediately). For 'run everything' intent (ى „ë¶€ ى§„ي–‰) instead of a curated â‰¤N batch. /cycle-fg + /cycle-bg (0.9.1) â€” STICKY EXECUTION-MODE TOGGLES. `/cycle-fg` sets the session marker `~/.sidecar/cycle-mode=fg` and runs the round FOREGROUND SEQUENTIAL (Stages 0-3 unchanged; Stage 4 executes each PROCEED row INLINE one-at-a-time with visible `â–¶ i/N â†’ âœ…/âڑ /â‌Œ` progress آ· NO Agent fan-out آ· NO parallelism آ· halt on failure آ· sbs-like bar). `/cycle-bg` sets the marker `=bg` and runs the round in the default BACKGROUND mode (parallel Agent fan-out + auto-continue to depletion). The marker is STICKY: after `/cycle-fg`, subsequent bare `/cycle` rounds ALSO run foreground until `/cycle-bg` flips back (and vice-versa). Bare `/cycle` reads the marker at its Stage -1 (default `bg` when absent) and dispatches to the matching execution mode. fg = debugging آ· careful review آ· cwd-shared / single-threaded-resource work; bg (default) = independent parallel work-bursts that need isolation. Both keep ALL guardrails (SSOT-freshness آ· dup-race SKIP آ· leak-sweep آ· resource-serialization). All safety/correctness guardrails are KEPT (SSOT-freshness, dup-race SKIP, leak-sweep, resource-contention serialization â€” fanning out everything still serializes EXCLUSIVE GPU resources, never parallel-fires every Agent â€” throttle-resilience, auto-continue to depletion). All commands share the 5-stage structure + plan-table + dup-race precheck + leak guardrails; they differ only in entry/pacing/cap/execution-mode shape. Distinct from all-bg-go (reactive fan-out of PRIOR-turn branches; /cycle self-generates the next batch each round). 0.8.0 fixes the per-round shape + adds THE LINTER (@D round_lint): every loop round ends with a result-report (status glyph + PR# + honest verify tier + 7-element easy + progress bar) â†’ debt/split tracking â†’ a `round-lint: N/10 âœ“` self-audit of the 10-point contract â†’ loop tail; plus @D oversized_split (â‰¥2 consecutive throttle deaths on one milestone â‡’ split into independently-landable sub-slices, never re-fire whole) + @D handoff_debt_ledger (cross-repo handoff goes to the host-local `sidecar handoff` registry â€” `sidecar handoff add <repo> <text>`, no target-tree write, so no uncommitted-edit debt). cycle-fg-loop/cycle-bg-loop/cycle-full-loop carry the fixed round shape. NL triggers: /cycle آ· /cycle-full آ· /cycle-fg-loop آ· /cycle-bg-loop آ· /cycle-full-loop آ· /cycle-all آ· /cycle-fg آ· /cycle-bg آ· ى‚¬ى‌´يپ´ آ· ê³„ى†چ ى§„ي–‰ آ· ë‹¤ى‌Œ ë‌¼ىڑ´ë“œ آ· keep cycling آ· march on آ· ى „ë¶€ ى§„ي–‰ آ· ى¶”ى²œ ë§گê³  ى „ë¶€ آ· ى‚¬ى‌´يپ´ يڈ¬ê·¸ë‌¼ىڑ´ë“œ آ· ى‚¬ى‌´يپ´ ىˆœى°¨ آ· ى‚¬ى‌´يپ´ ى§پى ‘ آ· foreground cycle آ· sequential cycle آ· inline cycle آ· ي•œ ë‹¨ê³„ى”© ى‚¬ى‌´يپ´ آ· ى‚¬ى‌´يپ´ ë°±ê·¸ë‌¼ىڑ´ë“œ آ· ى‚¬ى‌´يپ´ ë³‘ë ¬ آ· background cycle آ· parallel cycle آ· bg ëھ¨ë“œë،œ آ· ى‚¬ى‌´يپ´ يڈ¬ê·¸ë‌¼ىڑ´ë“œ ë£¨ي”„ آ· fg loop آ· ى—°ى†چ ىˆœى°¨ ى‚¬ى‌´يپ´ آ· ى‚¬ى‌´يپ´ ë°±ê·¸ë‌¼ىڑ´ë“œ ë£¨ي”„ آ· bg loop آ· ى—°ى†چ ë³‘ë ¬ ى‚¬ى‌´يپ´. 0.9.2 cross-links the /cycle-bg vs /micro-exp build-vs-fire decision tree (domain-agnostic; mirrored in skills/micro-exp/SKILL.md @D micro_exp_handoff): /cycle-bg is the canonical BUILD lane for candidate orchestration (build+fire integrated per worktree agent), /micro-exp is the FIRE-only lane (pre-built infra assumed; /micro-exp's Stage 1.5 auto-halts back to /cycle-bg on any missing prereq). Tree (kind-abstract): NO matrix â†’ /kick آ· matrix + ALL infra ready â†’ /micro-exp آ· matrix + missing infra â†’ /cycle-bg <domain> then /micro-exp.

plugins.description: /quota:quota <args> â€” Claude account 5h/7d usage limits + multi-account registry + live credential swap + per-account nicknames. Verbs: list (= status = bare آ· UNIFIED all-accounts table in chat, 6-col English header: Nickname آ· Email آ· Session Used آ· Session Reset آ· Weekly Used آ· Weekly Reset, â–“â–‘ 5-slot bar + humanized time-to-reset; rows sorted by nickname (else email), case-insensitive; the active account is âک… + fetched LIVE (45s cache), every other account shows its last per-account cache, `â€”` = never fetched) آ· all (same table, every account fetched LIVE in one shot, in PARALLEL â€” the easy toggle to 'refresh everything') آ· add [<nick>] (snapshot account + capture credentials into backup store آ· optional nickname like claude1) آ· nick <ref> [<nick>] (set or clear a nickname for an existing account; bare nick arg = clear) آ· switch <ref> (live cred swap, verified + rollback on failure) آ· remove <ref> (rm آ· delete آ· del â€” drop registry row + backup creds, leaves live ~/.claude.json untouched) آ· refresh [<ref>] (re-fetch usage into per-account cache; renews expired tokens via refreshToken) آ· autoadd (silent SessionStart-hook path â€” registers the active account iff not yet in the registry; driven by the quota-autoadd hook plugin so a fresh `claude /login` self-registers next session) آ· help. The view also auto-registers the live account if untracked (autoadd parity). <ref> = nickname (e.g. claude1) آ· email آ· numeric index â€” lookup order indexâ†’nickâ†’email. Live fetch via OAuth usage endpoint (api.anthropic.com/api/oauth/usage) + auto refresh-token grant (platform.claude.com/v1/oauth/token); 45s per-account cache. Per-account OAuth blob backup in macOS keychain svc 'sidecar-quota' / linux ~/.sidecar/quota/creds/<email>.json (0600 + atomic write). own-accounts-serial only â€” never a multi-tenant router (ToS red-line). Agent SDK credit (dual-bucket since 2026-06-15) is documented as future direction; current release tracks subscription 5h/7d only. The bare/list view renders from cache instantly then spawns a detached background stale-while-revalidate refresh of stale non-active accounts (cache > 300s, with backup creds) so the NEXT view trends fresh without an explicit `all` â€” the view never blocks on the network; a dead/expired backup token is skipped for a 1800s cooldown (shows `â€”` until `refresh <ref>` renews it); the active account keeps a 45s inline live refresh. Both `all` and the background cycle fetch their account set in parallel (`_parallel_refresh` â€” token-prep sequential, usage GETs concurrent). âڑ  uses non-public Anthropic OAuth endpoints â€” may break if rotated.

Verbs: /domain <NAME> (SELECT the session's active domain â†’ later verbs default to it + show a final-goal progress bar `â–“â–“â–“â–‘â–‘ NN% آ· done/total` from the snapshot's checkboxes) آ· /domain (show active) آ· /domain list (alias ls â€” repo-wide index; with a DOMAINS.tape roster it is AUTHORITATIVE: tables each registered domain âک… = active آ· @goal آ· progress آ· location, progress/@goal stay DERIVED so the checked-in roster holds only NAMEâ†’path and never churns; flags ghosts + unregistered; no roster â†’ legacy disk-scan) آ· /domain list --sync (reconcile DOMAINS.tape with disk â€” bootstraps the roster) آ· /domain <task> (append `- [x]` to log) آ· /domain todo <task> (`- [ ]`) آ· /domain done <match> (flip [ ]â†’[x]) آ· /domain title <text> (alias subtitle â€” OPTIONAL `@title:` display header: icon آ· name آ· alias, e.g. `ًں§  IIT4 â€” ى‌کى‹‌ ى¸،ى •ى‍گ(ه°؛)`, rendered in place of plain `active domain: NAME`; absence keeps current output, no lint warning) آ· /domain new <header>. plugins.name: worktree-gc
plugins.description: SessionStart hook that prunes merged-but-undeleted LINKED git worktrees in the cwd repo, implemented in hexa-lang (`_worktree_gc.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Threshold gate (linked-count >= 3 OR disk avail < 20 GiB) keeps the common case (1-2 worktrees, ample disk) silent. Above threshold, fetches `gh pr list --state merged --limit 200` and considers a worktree only when its branch is in that merged set OR its HEAD commit truly landed on origin/main. 0.2.0 adds FOUR unconditional live-work guards so an active worktree is NEVER wiped mid-task: (a) dirty-tree (uncommitted changes â†’ SKIP), (b) recent-mtime (HEAD commit or any file modified < 1h â†’ SKIP), (c) cwd-in-use (a live process' cwd is in the worktree, via lsof â†’ SKIP), (d) HEAD-ancestor real-merged check (`git merge-base --is-ancestor <wt-HEAD> origin/main`) â€” a vanished `origin/<branch>` ref alone is IGNORED so a reused branch name can't condemn fresh work. (e) Atomic prune: `git worktree remove` is the sole removal path; on failure NOTHING is deleted (no partial / manual rm). Open-PR worktrees are NEVER touched. gh unavailable / no merged PRs â†’ short note + no action. Always exits 0 (non-blocking). NO opt-out by design.

NO opt-out by design (project.tape @D s11) â€” no env var / config disable switch; uninstall the plugin for manual control.

plugins.name: s9-guard

Emits non-blocking systemMessage reminding the agent that any conclusion drawn from raw load must EXCLUDE `claude` PIDs (claude sessions are exempt from local load attribution) and suggesting the canonical claude-excluded sum (`ps -Ao pcpu,comm | awk '$2 !~ /claude/ {s+=$1} END {print int(s)}'`). NEVER blocks â€” pure advisory. Implemented in hexa-lang (`_s9_guard.hexa`, invoked via `hexa run`). Ships per @D s7 (governance + enforcement together) â€” the @D s9 companion guard. NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: askq-text

A PreToolUse hook cannot re-render the box as text (no transform API), so the hook DENIES the call with an instruction telling the model to re-emit the SAME question as plain text in its reply (state it in prose, list any options inline marking a recommendation, accept a free-form answer). plugins.name: lsp-rebuild
plugins.description: PostToolUse(Write|Edit) auto-rebuild for hexa-native LSP servers, in hexa-lang (`_lsp_rebuild.hexa`, via `hexa run`). Keeps the build artifact in lockstep with the canonical .hexa grammar SSOT so a grammar change auto-propagates without a manual rebuild. Rebuild-not-source-run because the hexa LSP stdio server needs the `read_stdin_n_c` FFI which only links into a COMPILED binary. Fires on the `*/lsp/*_lsp.hexa` pattern AND on hexa-lang's own LSP (`self/lsp.hexa` monolith + `self/lsp/*.hexa` submodules â€” NOT named `*_lsp.hexa`), rebuilt via its `tool/build_hexa_lsp.hexa` orchestrator â†’ `<repo>/bin/hexa-lsp` (project.tape @D s11). Pairs with the `*-lsp` wiring plugins (hexa-lsp آ· kosmos-lsp آ· n6-lsp آ· hxc-lsp). NO opt-out by design.

plugins.name: msg
plugins.description: /msg â€” session-to-session messaging over a UNIX domain socket, NO MCP (sidecar @D s15). Two Claude Code TUI sessions exchange one-line JSON messages: the SEND side is a CLI one-liner (`socat - UNIX-CONNECT:<peer>.sock`, fallback `nc -U <path>`); the RECEIVE side is the agent arming a persistent Monitor â€” phase-1 directly on `socat UNIX-LISTEN:<self>.sock,fork -`, OR, when the `msg-arm` hook's capture daemon owns the socket and durably captures to `<self>.inbox.log`, on `tail -f <self>.inbox.log` (phase-2 coexistence â€” `listen` auto-retargets) â€” so each received line surfaces as a chat task-notification. Local mailbox + roster under `~/.sidecar/msg/` (roster.json maps nick -> socket path + sid + ts) so pool-route keeps it LOCAL. Verbs â€” `send <nick> <text...>` (direct) آ· `broadcast <text...>` (= `send all <text>`) آ· `listen` (upsert self + print the Monitor command, retargeting to the inbox log when msg-arm is present) آ· `who` (roster table, alive marked via `test -S`, dead rows flagged not deleted) آ· `nick <name>` آ· bare `/msg` (status). NL triggers via the skill â€” 'ى„¸ى…کëپ¼ë¦¬ ي†µى‹ ' آ· 'ىک† ى„¸ى…کى—گ ë³´ë‚´' آ· 'send to session' آ· 'socket message' آ· 'inter-session'.

plugins.name: msg-arm
plugins.description: SessionStart + UserPromptSubmit hook â€” phase-2 AUTO-RECEIVE for the `msg` plugin, NO MCP (sidecar @D s15). A UNIX socket is a connection not a queue, so phase-1 `/msg listen` drops every message that arrives while no one is bound. msg-arm closes the gap: on SessionStart it upserts self into `~/.sidecar/msg/roster.json` and spawns a DETACHED capture daemon (`socat UNIX-LISTEN:<self>.sock,fork OPEN:<self>.inbox.log,append,create`, fallback `nc -lkU`; setsidâ†’nohup) that OWNS the socket and appends every received line to `<self>.inbox.log` â€” idempotent via a `<self>.daemon.pid` pidfile + pgrep guard, stale socket `rm -f` before bind. On SessionStart + every UserPromptSubmit it drains `<self>.inbox.log` from the byte offset in `<self>.offset`, emits new lines as additionalContext (`ًں“¨ from X: ...`, capped ~20 with an overflow summary), and advances the offset (resets to 0 on truncation/rotation). Self nick from `~/.sidecar/msg/self` (shared SSOT with the `msg` command plugin). NO opt-out switch (s11) â€” gates only on real preconditions (nick set + socat|nc present), else graceful exit 0 (never blocks session start). hexa-lang (`_msg_arm.hexa`). Pairs with `msg` 0.2.0 whose `listen` retargets to `tail -f <self>.inbox.log` when the daemon owns the socket.

plugins.name: msg-mcp
plugins.description: msg phase-3 IDLE-PUSH â€” an HTTP/SSE-transport MCP channels server (sidecar @D s15 1.8 allows HTTP/SSE MCP; STDIO MCP stays forbidden). msg(0.2.0) + msg-arm(0.1.0) capture inbound session-to-session lines into `~/.sidecar/msg/<self>.inbox.log`, but an idle session only drains them on its NEXT turn (poll). This plugin closes that gap: a single local Node broker (Streamable HTTP on 127.0.0.1:7717) tails `<self>.inbox.log` from a dedicated `<self>.mcp.offset` (separate from msg-arm's `.offset` so the two never contend) and, per new JSON line `{from,text,ts}`, emits an MCP channel notification `method:notifications/claude/channel` `params:{content:"ًں“¨ from <nick>: <text>"}` â€” which Claude Code PUSHES into the live-but-idle session (no turn needed). The server declares the `experimental["claude/channel"]` capability the host requires; plugin.json exposes it as a `channels` entry + an http url-based `mcpServers` entry (NOT stdio command-based â†’ lint passes). receive-PUSH only â€” send stays the existing `/msg send` socket path (no re-implementation). SessionStart fires a detached daemon-launcher (`_msg_mcp_up.hexa`; pidfile + pgrep guard, msg-arm pattern; on first run `npm install --omit=dev @modelcontextprotocol/sdk` into the plugin dir if node_modules absent â€” node_modules never committed). sidecar's FIRST non-hexa (Node) executable. IDLE-PUSH requires Claude Code launched with `--dangerously-load-development-channels msg-mcp` (or `--channels msg-mcp` once GA) AND a first-party provider (Bedrock/Vertex skip) â€” experimental research-preview. NO opt-out switch (s11) â€” gates only on real preconditions (node present آ· nick set), else graceful exit 0. NO absolute paths ($HOME/$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT only).
